channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Michael Jackson Biopic's Success Amidst Controversy
item.description: The recently released Michael Jackson biopic, despite ongoing controversies surrounding abuse allegations, appears to be doing quite well for the franchise. Reports indicate that the film's performanc...

item.title: Oburu Odinga consolidates political influence at Pawaa Centre
item.description: Dr. Oburu Oginga, Raila Odinga’s elder brother, has been steadily tightening his grip on the structures of power surrounding the Pawaa Centre, a political hub long associated with the Odinga family. T...

item.title: South Africa Introduces Points-Based Immigration System to Prioritise Economic Contribution
item.description: South Africa has approved a revised immigration policy framework aimed at tightening controls while promoting economic growth. The Updated White Paper on Citizenship, Immigration and Refugee Protectio...

item.title: Naira remains stable against Dollar as of April 10, 2026
item.description: The Naira has continued to hold steady against the United States Dollar as of April 10, 2026. In the early trading hours, the Nigerian Foreign Exchange Market (NFEM) saw the Naira open at approximatel...

item.title: Harivansh Narayan Singh nominated to Rajya Sabha after Ranjan Gogoi's retirement
item.description: In a significant update for India's Parliament, President Droupadi Murmu has nominated Harivansh Narayan Singh to the Rajya Sabha to fill the vacancy left by the retirement of former Chief Justice of ...

item.title: Supreme Court’s review of the Sabarimala women entry case explained
item.description: The Supreme Court of India reviewed the long-standing Sabarimala temple entry case, which revolves around the right of women of menstruating age to enter the shrine dedicated to Lord Ayyappa in Kerala...

item.title: Nigerian Poultry Farmers Adapt to Rising Feed Costs
item.description: Nigerian poultry farmers are facing a tough time as the cost of maize and soybean, key ingredients in poultry feed, has skyrocketed. To survive and keep their businesses afloat, many farmers are now e...

item.title: Aga Khan Museum highlights the cultural connections between sports, games, and art
item.description: The Aga Khan Museum in Toronto is launching an exhibition titled 'Game On!', which explores the fusion of sports, games, and artistic creativity across cultures and historical periods. Curated by Bita...

item.title: Ruto Vows to Tackle Cartel Influence in Nairobi
item.description: President William Ruto has put his foot down, promis' to crack down on the cartels that have been causin' problems in Nairobi. He addressed the Nairobi County Assembly and basically said enough is eno...

item.title: Ladysmith Black Mambazo marks 65 years with a collaborative album celebrating unity and heritage
item.description: Ladysmith Black Mambazo has unveiled their new album ‘One Voice One World’, a milestone project that unites multiple generations of South African musical talent. The legendary isicathamiya group colla...

item.title: B.C. man sentenced to under seven years for manslaughter in shooting death of wife, with Indigenous background as mitigating factor
item.description: Brent McCook, a 28-year-old Indigenous man from the remote Tsek’ehne First Nations community of Kwadacha in northern British Columbia, was sentenced to six years and nine months in prison for the mans...

item.title: Cruise Lines Expand Horizons: Land Extensions Reach Remote Destinations
item.description: Cruise lines are dramatically expanding their offerings to include land extensions, allowing travellers to experience destinations previously inaccessible from a typical cruise itinerary. This trend, ...

item.title: Tamil Nadu CM M K Stalin engages with commuters during Chennai Metro ride as part of election campaign
item.description: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M K Stalin made an unexpected visit on the Chennai Metro as part of his campaign ahead of the upcoming Assembly elections scheduled for April 23. He travelled from Chennai Ce...

item.title: Trump’s 2025 Trade Agenda: How Big Tech Shaped Global Deals
item.description: The Crikey report examines how US President Donald Trump’s administration has reshaped global trade policy to advance the interests of major American technology companies. Since taking office in Janua...

item.title: Asian Development Bank Lowers Philippines GDP Forecast Amid Middle East Conflict
item.description: The Asian Development Bank (ADB) has revised its economic growth outlook for the Philippines downward, citing the ongoing conflict in the Middle East as a significant external risk. According to ADB a...

item.title: Jim Jarmusch Reflects on His Seven-Year Journey Creating 'Father Mother Sister Brother'
item.description: Jim Jarmusch, aged 73, remains a defining figure in independent American cinema, known for his idiosyncratic storytelling and character-driven narratives. His new film, 'Father Mother Sister Brother',...

item.title: Strait of Hormuz ka traffic ab bhi 90 percent kam hai, Iran ke control ke neeche chal raha hai
item.description: Strait of Hormuz se guzre hue ships ka traffic ab bhi apni normal level se 90 percent kam hai, chahe US aur Iran ke beech mein ceasefire ho gaya ho. Iran ke Revolutionary Guard (IRGC) ne ships ko Lara...

item.title: White House Reacts to Melania Trump’s Unexpected Comments About Jeffrey Epstein

item.title: Current Dollar to Naira Exchange Rates for April 10, 2026

item.title: Afrika Bambaataa, Hip Hop Pioneer and Zulu Nation Founder, Passes Away at 68
item.description: Afrika Bambaataa, the influential hip hop pioneer and founder of the Universal Zulu Nation, has passed away at the age of 68. Bambaataa, born in New York City in 1957, played a crucial role in the dev...
